word_from:
## North American Immersion
### Why attend an english language school in North America?
With a 4.8-star rating across 195 student reviews, choosing the right english language school defines your study abroad trip. Results matter. Our english language school operates accredited campuses in San Diego, California, Los Angeles, California, and Vancouver, British Columbia. As an institution recognized by the Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA), we pair small class sizes with highly qualified teachers to build real fluency. Students from Brazil, Switzerland, South Korea, and dozens of other nations arrive every Tuesday to begin their studies. You will practice conversation naturally while living near beaches or busy downtown centers. Small groups mean your teachers know your name and your personal goals. The focus remains strictly on practical communication, pronunciation, and listening comprehension. We designed these locations to give you immediate access to local culture outside the classroom.
word_to:
## Soggiorno linguistico nel Nord America
### Perché frequentare una scuola di lingua in Nord America?
Con una valutazione di 4,8 stelle su 195 recensioni di studenti, scegliere la giusta scuola di lingua definisce il Suo soggiorno linguistico. I risultati contano. La nostra scuola di lingua gestisce campus accreditati a San Diego (California), Los Angeles (California) e Vancouver, in Columbia Britannica. In quanto istituzione riconosciuta dalla Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA), abbiniamo classi poco numerose a insegnanti altamente qualificati per costruire una reale padronanza della lingua. Studenti provenienti da Brasile, Svizzera, Corea del Sud e dozzine di altre nazioni arrivano ogni martedì per iniziare i loro studi. Praticherà la conversazione in modo naturale vivendo vicino alle spiagge o nei vivaci centri cittadini. I piccoli gruppi permettono agli insegnanti di conoscere il Suo nome e i Suoi obiettivi personali. L'attenzione rimane strettamente rivolta alla comunicazione pratica, alla pronuncia e alla comprensione orale. Abbiamo strutturato queste sedi per offrirLe un accesso immediato alla cultura locale fuori dall'aula.

word_from:
#### How do campus locations shape your daily routine?
A great environment speeds up learning at any english language school. Location matters. Students taking classes in San Diego often head straight to Pacific Beach to surf or play volleyball after school. Those in Santa Monica enjoy the California sunshine just blocks from the ocean. ## Esperienza in California per ragazzi
### Che cos'è la vacanza studio CEL a San Diego?

word_from: CEL's English summer camp in San Diego is a residential English-language program for teenagers aged 14 to 18. This specialized track combines morning classes at the Downtown San Diego campus with afternoon and weekend excursions across Southern California. Accredited by the Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA), the program groups international students into small classes averaging 7 participants, capped at 12. The camp runs annually from late June into early August; the 2025 session takes place from June 28 to August 9. Students benefit from a structured [English language program](https://www.englishcollege.com/courses) that integrates rigorous academic preparation with supervised homestay accommodation. By blending classroom instruction with cultural activities, the curriculum ensures rapid skill acquisition. #### Why do small classes at an english language school accelerate fluency?
The math is simple. Fewer students mean more speaking time. When you attend an english language school that caps enrollment at 12 people per room, you cannot hide in the back row. You must speak. You must answer questions. This constant engagement builds confidence rapidly. The Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA) recognizes facilities across the industry that maintain high academic standards, and rigorous teacher feedback is a core part of that success. Your instructors catch your recurring mistakes immediately. They correct your pronunciation before it becomes a habit. This is why our students consistently report clear, measurable improvement week over week. They leave ready to navigate the world.

word_from: How long does it take to learn English in Vancouver?
word_to: Quanto tempo serve per imparare l'inglese a Vancouver?

word_from: Most international students studying English in Vancouver advance one Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) level every 10 weeks. This baseline assumes full-time enrollment and consistent daily practice. At our campus in Vancouver, British Columbia, we keep class sizes small. You share the room with an average of 7 students, and never more than 12. This structure allows our certified teachers to provide direct feedback on your pronunciation and grammar. As a recognized Designated Learning Institution (DLI #O19395677113), CEL tracks your progress using strict international benchmarks from A1 beginner to C2 mastery. Moving from a B1 intermediate to a B2 upper-intermediate level generally requires about 250 hours of guided instruction. Your exact timeline depends heavily on how much you interact with locals outside of class hours. Choosing an immersive environment accelerates your learning curve significantly. Students who actively participate in weekend activities or join local conversation clubs routinely reach their target fluency faster than those who only speak English during lessons.
word_to: La maggior parte degli studenti internazionali che studiano l'inglese a Vancouver avanza di un livello del Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) ogni 10 settimane. Questa stima di base presuppone un'iscrizione a tempo pieno e una pratica quotidiana costante. Presso il nostro campus di Vancouver, in British Columbia, manteniamo classi di dimensioni ridotte. Condividerà l'aula con una media di 7 studenti, e mai più di 12. Questa struttura permette ai nostri insegnanti certificati di fornire un riscontro diretto sulla pronuncia e sulla grammatica. Essendo un Designated Learning Institution (DLI #O19395677113) riconosciuto, CEL monitora i Suoi progressi utilizzando rigorosi parametri internazionali, dal livello A1 per principianti fino alla padronanza del C2. Passare da un livello B1 intermedio a un B2 intermedio-alto richiede solitamente circa 250 ore di istruzione guidata. Le Sue tempistiche esatte dipendono fortemente da quanto interagisce con le persone del posto al di fuori dell'orario di lezione. Scegliere un ambiente di immersione linguistica accelera notevolmente la curva di apprendimento. Chi partecipa attivamente alle attività del fine settimana o si unisce a gruppi di conversazione locali raggiunge regolarmente la fluidità desiderata più rapidamente rispetto a chi parla inglese solo durante le lezioni.

word_from: Short programs ranging from one to four weeks provide a helpful introduction to the city. They build immediate speaking confidence and refresh existing vocabulary. You will navigate coffee shops and public transit with ease. However, meaningful academic progression takes time. If your goal involves taking the International English Language Testing System (IELTS) exam, you should expect to spend at least 12 to 20 weeks preparing. Many professionals studying English in Vancouver combine morning classes with afternoon excursions to maximize their exposure to authentic Canadian accents.

word_from: Do beginners learn faster than advanced students?
word_to: I principianti imparano più velocemente degli studenti avanzati?

word_from: Yes. Starting from zero feels incredibly fast. An absolute beginner at the A1 stage can pick up basic vocabulary and simple sentence structures in just four weeks. Going from no knowledge to ordering coffee successfully feels like a massive leap. You see immediate results. Once you reach a B2 level, the vocabulary becomes highly specific and the grammar nuances get trickier. Moving from upper-intermediate to advanced proficiency takes longer because you are refining complex arguments rather than exchanging basic information. International students often feel frustrated during this stage, but the slow refinement is completely normal. Consistent exposure to English in Vancouver helps push past this intermediate plateau.

Una volta raggiunto un livello B2, il vocabolario diventa molto specifico e le sfumature grammaticali si fanno più insidiose. Il passaggio da una competenza intermedio-alta a una avanzata richiede più tempo. Lei starà affinando argomentazioni complesse piuttosto che scambiando informazioni di base. ## City Comparison
### Vancouver vs Toronto: Which City Fits Your Language Goals?
word_to:
## Confronto tra le città
### Vancouver o Toronto: quale città risponde ai Suoi obiettivi linguistici?

word_from: When weighing Vancouver vs Toronto for your language studies, the best choice depends heavily on your daily lifestyle preferences and academic needs. Vancouver, British Columbia, offers a mild climate, oceanfront access, and an average class size of seven students at our school. This makes it an excellent fit for those wanting measurable academic progression. Toronto provides a bustling metropolitan environment with 6.2 million residents and a major financial hub, ideal for corporate networking. Both cities are premier destinations for students who want to [learn English in Canada](https://www.englishcollege.com/learn-english-canada), making the Vancouver vs Toronto comparison essential. At the [College of English Language (CEL)](https://www.englishcollege.com/), we prioritize personalized feedback and structured progression aligned with the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR). Our location allows you to bypass the typical 15-student large-school model, giving you more direct speaking time. Deciding between these two distinct environments requires looking closely at how you want to spend your time outside the classroom.
word_to: Nel valutare il confronto tra Vancouver e Toronto per il Suo soggiorno linguistico, la scelta migliore dipende molto dallo stile di vita desiderato e dalle esigenze accademiche. Vancouver, in Columbia Britannica, offre un clima mite, l'accesso diretto all'oceano e classi con una media di sette studenti nella nostra scuola. Tali caratteristiche la rendono perfetta per ottenere progressi accademici misurabili. Toronto presenta invece un ambiente metropolitano molto vivace con 6,2 milioni di abitanti e un enorme polo finanziario, l'ideale per creare una rete di contatti professionali. Entrambe le città rappresentano mete d'eccellenza per gli studenti che desiderano [studiare l'inglese in Canada](https://www.englishcollege.com/it/soggiorni-linguistici-canada); per questo il confronto tra Vancouver e Toronto risulta molto utile. Presso il [College of English Language (CEL)](https://www.englishcollege.com/it) diamo priorità a valutazioni personalizzate e a un percorso ben strutturato in linea con il Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR). La nostra sede consente di evitare il classico modello delle grandi scuole con quindici studenti per classe. In questo modo Lei avrà molto più tempo per parlare direttamente in lingua. La scelta tra questi due ambienti richiede un'attenta analisi di come si desidera trascorrere il tempo libero al di fuori delle lezioni.

word_from: Beyond class size, the climate dramatically shapes your experience. The fast-paced corporate culture of the east coast contrasts sharply with the balanced, outdoor-oriented rhythm of the west coast. This means your personal priorities act as the ultimate deciding factor in the Vancouver vs Toronto debate.

word_from: How does the climate shape your study experience?

word_from: When choosing Vancouver vs Toronto, weather is often the first major consideration. Students who dislike freezing temperatures typically gravitate toward the west coast. The city experiences mild winters and moderate temperatures throughout the year. You can ski in the nearby mountains and walk along the ocean in the exact same week. Rain is common, especially in winter, but extreme cold is rare. Evaluating Vancouver vs Toronto weather profiles reveals a stark contrast. The eastern metropolis features four distinct seasons. You will experience cold, snowy winters and hot, humid summers. This clear seasonal contrast appeals to students who want a traditional winter experience alongside their language studies.

Accredited by the Accrediting Council for Continuing Education and Training (ACCET) in the United States and Languages Canada, this foundation program serves learners aiming for baseline fluency rather than immediate university entry. Classes focus on the speaking, listening, reading, and writing skills necessary for daily life in San Diego, California, Los Angeles, California, or Vancouver, British Columbia. Instructors adapt weekly topics directly based on the immediate needs of the room. Absolute beginners start with essential survival phrases. Advanced students often debate global news. Teachers tailor the syllabus so students can confidently navigate real-world situations, from ordering food to understanding casual conversation. Yes. This is exactly where most students begin before moving to specialized tracks.

word_from: Choosing the english plus surfing program means combining rigorous morning academics with afternoon coastal action. Most students who arrive at our [San Diego language school](https://www.englishcollege.com/san-diego-ca/language-school) in California reach a solid working B2 level on the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) in 12 weeks of full-time study. The catch: that timeline assumes you attend all 20 morning lessons per week and genuinely engage with your afternoon wave sessions. Location matters immensely for this track. A daily schedule that involves the ocean requires consistent waves and highly accessible beaches. Our campus provides exactly that environment. You spend your mornings focused on grammar, speaking, reading, and listening. After a short lunch break, you grab a board. The physical activity acts as a reset button for your mind. Yes, really. Students consistently report that they retain more vocabulary when they step out of the traditional classroom and use their new skills physically.
word_to: Scegliere il programma english plus surfing significa combinare uno studio mattutino rigoroso con l'attività pomeridiana sulla costa. La maggior parte degli studenti che arrivano nella nostra [scuola di lingua a San Diego](https://www.englishcollege.com/it/san-diego-ca/scuole-di-lingue), in California, raggiunge un solido livello B2 del Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) in 12 settimane di studio a tempo pieno. Il punto è questo: tale tempistica presume che Lei frequenti tutte le 20 lezioni mattutine a settimana e partecipi attivamente alle sessioni pomeridiane sulle onde. La posizione fa davvero la differenza per questo percorso. Un programma giornaliero che coinvolge l'oceano richiede onde costanti e spiagge facilmente accessibili. Il nostro campus offre esattamente questo ambiente. Trascorrerà le mattine concentrandosi su grammatica, conversazione, lettura e ascolto. Dopo una breve pausa pranzo, prenderà la tavola. L'attività fisica funziona come un pulsante di riavvio per la mente. Davvero. Gli studenti riferiscono costantemente di memorizzare più vocaboli quando escono dalla classe tradizionale e usano le nuove competenze fisicamente.

word_from: Paddling out with classmates forces you to communicate over the sound of the ocean. You shout warnings, encourage each other, and ask your coach for feedback in real-time. This program is not just an extended vacation. It maintains strict academic standards. The College of English Language holds dual accreditation from the Accrediting Council for Continuing Education and Training (ACCET) and the Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA). This means your morning classes meet high institutional standards. You receive exactly the same core instruction as students enrolled in [General English](https://www.englishcollege.com/courses/general-english) taking a purely academic route, but your english plus surfing itinerary shifts entirely in the afternoon.

Ci si scambia avvertimenti, ci si incoraggia a vicenda e si chiedono consigli all'istruttore in tempo reale. Questo programma non è una semplice vacanza prolungata. Mantiene standard accademici severi. Il College of English Language possiede il doppio accreditamento da parte dell'Accrediting Council for Continuing Education and Training (ACCET) e della Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA). Questo significa che le Sue lezioni mattutine soddisfano requisiti istituzionali elevati. Riceverà la stessa identica istruzione di base degli studenti iscritti a [General English](https://www.englishcollege.com/it/corsi/general-english) che seguono un percorso puramente accademico, ma il Suo itinerario english plus surfing cambia completamente nel pomeriggio.

word_from: Many students use the english plus surfing track as a productive mental break between more intense study periods. A common progression involves starting with 4 weeks of standard study to adjust to the timezone and culture. You might then spend 8 weeks focusing on the ocean. Finally, students aiming for university admission often transition into academic pathways targeting [undergraduate programs](https://www.englishcollege.com/pathway/undergraduate-programs). It works. The physical exertion of the afternoon sessions actually helps process the morning grammar lessons.

When you fall off a board and laugh with your instructor, you stop worrying about perfect verb conjugation. You just speak. Advanced students benefit from learning specialized vocabulary and natural local expressions that rarely appear in textbooks.

Many students plan to attend university in North America. They need formal proof of their language abilities. Students who explore our engaging course tracks for higher education often choose specific electives. Preparation is available for the Test of English as a Foreign Language internet-Based Test (TOEFL iBT). You can also study for Cambridge English: First (FCE) and Cambridge English: Advanced (CAE). The International English Language Testing System (IELTS) is another common benchmark. You need an upper intermediate level to join these exam classes. Teachers help you understand the specific test structures. They review practice materials and teach time management strategies. Students aiming for college degrees can also take the [Global Pathway Preparation program](https://www.englishcollege.com/courses/global-pathway-preparation). This specific track builds academic skills for college success. It focuses on research, essay writing, and academic presentations.
